title: New Store Opening Intelligence
description: TinyFish Layer 1 schema — detects new retail stores that have recently opened OR are opening soon in a target area, then evaluates whether that co-tenancy pattern signals a good location for opening a similar store. Logic: if Trader Joe's just opened nearby, grocery demand is validated. If a 5th Subway just opened, the area is saturated.
_tinyfish_agent_goal: You are a retail site selection analyst. For the target city/neighborhood, find ALL new store openings from the past 6 months AND announced future openings. For each, determine: (1) Is this a demand validator (proves the area has viable retail demand for this category)? (2) Is this a saturation signal (so many stores of the same type that a new entrant would struggle)? (3) Is the opening a reaction to organic demand (proof consumers already exist) or speculative (first mover risk)? Return structured data. Be specific — include store names, addresses if available, and opening dates.
_tinyfish_search_queries: "[CITY]" new retail store opening announcement 2025 site:businessinsider.com OR site:Reuters.com OR site:prnewswire.com
site:loopnet.com "[CITY]" retail lease signed OR "new tenant" 2025

site_recommendation.description: Top-level verdict: given the new store activity, is this a GOOD or BAD place to open YOUR store?

verdict.description: strong_yes = multiple validators, high demand, low saturation. no = already oversaturated or wrong demographic.

confidence.description: How confident is this verdict? 0.9+ = clear signal, 0.5 = mixed signals, 0.3 = insufficient data.

reasoning.description: 2–4 sentence explanation. E.g.: 'Whole Foods + Trader Joe's both opened within a year = premium grocery demand validated. Your mid-range grocery store would capture the gap between them. However, 3 existing Krogers create price competition risk.'

demand_validation_score.description: How strongly do the new openings PROVE that consumer demand exists for this category? 80+ = multiple large retailers committing capital here.

saturation_risk_score.description: How saturated is this specific retail category in this area? 70+ = too many competitors already.

new_store_openings.description: All new stores that have opened or announced openings in the past 6 months.

is_demand_validator.description: true if this opening proves there is consumer demand for this retail category in the area

is_direct_competitor.description: true if this store would directly compete with the retailer being analyzed

co_tenancy_attractiveness.description: How much does having this store nearby HELP your store? 0.9 = Trader Joe's next to your organic cafe is great. 0.1 = direct competitor hurts you.

opening_velocity.description: Rate of new store openings — fast velocity = growing market.

net_new_stores.description: openings_last_6_months minus closures_last_6_months. Positive = growing market.

velocity_label.description: boom=5+ net new/month, growth=2-4, stable=0-1, contraction=negative, collapse=many closures

category_saturation_map.description: How saturated is each retail category in this area? Key = category name, Value = saturation (0-100).

whitespace_opportunities.description: Retail categories that have DEMAND signals but NO or FEW new openings = untapped opportunity.

evidence.description: Why is this a whitespace? E.g. 'Lots of young families moving in, but no Target or Walmart within 8 miles'
